reviews.review_text: Very happy with park toilet showers very clean
Great stay in a cabin at Jubilee Lake. Peaceful, clean and beautiful place ,would definitely come again.

We stayed in a caravan, as we were in the region for a workshop. The park was clean, the surroundings beautiful, and a lovely short walk around the beautiful lake. The park was very particular about no noise past 11pm which was much appreciated too. Everyone around the park was respectful and lovely and staff are very helpful. Well worth a visit! Bonus is the region is beautiful and the location is central for many diverse activities.

On arrival none of the Dishes had been washed from the previous occupant. Room also had a very bad condensation problem.
No Fan or window in Bathroom.

We have just spent 3 nights at Jubilee Lake Holiday Park, we had a great time sites are big with a great view, amenities block always clean and serviced regularly, great communication from managers prior to arrival and after departing. Would highly recommend this park.

We enjoyed our stay in cabin 7, the only thing I would ask, is to lower the hedge to sitting height on the patio, we could not see the wildlife walking pass or the early wallabies that we seen further in the distance, apart from that, all was tranquil, nice to listen to the birds, thank you
Friendly and helpful staff.  Clean toilets and showers. Nice quiet location.

Had a lovely short stay camping.
Was short notice very lucky to get in.
Loved all the wildlife wandering around.
Lake and short walks were gorgeous with the autumn leaves. Toilet showers and camp kitchen was very clean and well kept. Was absolutely fantastic with a fire to keep warm.
Thankyou will definitely be back again.
Always amazing camping location

Very beautiful area with lots of space and great walks to go on.
Rooms were clean, as were the amenities.
Great hospitable staff who do their best to look after you, Mark was especially helpful with assisting us with a dead battery and was very friendly
Another fabulous Easter spent at Jubilee. Such a peaceful beautiful park with lovely clean amenities. Darren has done a wonderful job of making some great improvements over the last couple of years.

Stayed a few nights in cabin 8 (dog friendly). Nice basic and clean. Bit old, but had everything we needed. Fenced in back porch, and in a very quiet and peaceful location. No oven, hair dryer, cheese grater, (I'm picky lol) but that's ok. Plenty of peacocks to annoy our overly reactive dog. Sooo much birdlife! Lots of good places for walks, and extremely friendly welcome from reception. Five minutes into Daylesford. Not swish, but perfect for a relaxing getaway. Would stay there again.

Beautiful location.  Lake is great to swim in.  Lost a star because the camp kitchen is pretty ordinary. Another star because the shower was just cold water at 9.30 at night.  Apparently they were warm in the morning so maybe I was just unlucky. Plenty of permanent vans there so maybe that's why the other services are a bit lax.

Gorgeous park. Quiet. Gorgeous lake. Wildlife everywhere. Beautiful natural setting. Close to everything including Hepburn springs

Clean ammenities
Very pleasant place and close to nature. Spring water and black berries are very good.

Beautiful well situated campground if you don’t mind the bugs! We were in scouts lane and there were plenty of mosquitos and March flies.
Lots of the unpowered camp spots are not level making it tricky to position yourself if you don’t want to sleep on an angle. 5+ minute walk to the nearest bathrooms which were well maintained. Experienced lovely staff at the front desk.

Stayed here with my dog, lovely lake to paddle around in, kiosk with some delicious homemade pies and the service is so friendly they came out to raise the umbrella where I was eating with the local peacocks that eat right out of your hand

lovely park,easy to navigate.
Clean ammenities
Super friendly staff.
Can highly recommend this place .

I've yet to try the room's however, I can personally, recommend this darling place but ONLY, IF your wanting a calm, peaceful location, seeing the occasional fisherman, including children n families, yet still far enough away for alone time, if that's your desire.

Plenty to do for all age's, really.
Lover's, young families, older peeps, n a lot of wildlife, including the majestic peacock's, water hen's n soooo many different bird's, including the obligatory kookaburras lol.

Absolutely stunning place IMO.
Much less tourist-ey, as well. Ssssshhhhhh!

They have comfy seating, with umbrella's for shade, but there are huge tree's everywhere, so you can stand or sit under those.

I even spotted a fellow artist, beside the lake, painting, as well as photographers, if that is your thing?

Even a measly 10 or 15 mins here, is way worth it but the longer the better. Ahhhh
Great to see!
If you enjoy with not only birds singing but also people who stays up chatting and  listening to music , this is the place for you.
Great place to visit
Nice location, very pleasant to stay with wildlife in mornings and later in the evening.

Stayed at one of their cabins. It's clean and well managed. Staff are friendly and helpful. Café is reasonably priced. Location is excellent with a beautiful lake and walking track s. I recommend family with kids and dogs staying there.
This place is great we have only been here for the afternoon so happy to be staying a few more nights

What a beautiful spot!
Cabins are well appointed, I didn't need for anything.
The staff are amazing and Stu makes an awesome coffee.
From the peacocks, to the lake, the water lilies, the beautiful walks, all made this a relaxing, peaceful getaway....and all just a short drive or walk even from main street Daylesford.
I will definitely come and stay again.

Thank you so much for your hospitality Daren and Stu.

Love the peacocks
Need new showers
The kitchen needs to be upgraded a game room will be nice.
A bit run down but ok for deal we had.

We just spent 3 nights in a caravan converted into a cabin with 2 adults and 2 kids - and it was terrific! The park has everything you could possibly need and is absolutely immaculate. Our accommodation was spotless, and the customer service was amazing ✨

We had a small issue with our cabin, but the incredible staff were onto it straight away, which we really appreciated.

Looking forward to returning soon! 🌿
Typical caravan/holiday park. Clean ablution block and kitchen. Lovely open spaces for kids to play and nearby lake for canoeing
Great caravan park , staff awesome and location is great 👍
Beautifully located caravan park. We stayed in an onsite van this time which was clean and comfortable.

The perfect family spot! Very friendly customer service and great facilities! They even have boat bicycles, a heated bathroom warmest ever! And a fishing area and playground and so many peacocks 🦚 👌🏕😍
A favourite of mine. Have stayed several times. Good staff. Grounds and walks are great. Can get busy as it’s based around a public park area.
Very nice and clean. Dads gully is our new go to for a dads camping trip!
Good place for walking, be careful the birds

The Jubilee Lake Holiday Park is an amazing place to spend overnight or for a week during peak or off-peak season. I thoroughly enjoyed my stay there. All the amenities were available.  The bed was so comfy. The room and bathroom were immaculately clean. Top notch. Please stay and support the local Daylesford Hepburn Springs Town's business. Highly Recommended ☆☆☆☆☆
I was told there was fish in the lake but didn't catch any. Might be a me issue. Very nice walking track around the lake. Peacock are everywhere! Including baby ones

Great people
Friendly place
Great experience
Great location for a quiet getaway.
We really enjoyed our stay with our dog. Woke up to frosty mornings and our dog loved her time here. The amenities were very clean we hope to return soon. Thanks for a great stay.

Nice location mainly with lots of old on site caravans/cabin accommodation . Clean ammenities too.  I booked for 3 nights but could only tolerate my noisy neighbours for 2 night. Had to leave. Noisy and no privacy. They may have been elderly as the tv volume was incredibly loud, as were their phone conversations to the kids and grandkids (heard them all !) I couldn’t understand that the caravan park would put us next to each other, when there were 8 other empty sites around us !!  I guess I could have asked to move but the park owners should display more commonsense when allocating sites. Just a comment, not a criticism.  Wouldn’t go back

Omggggg this place was so much fun!!!
Went with my family friends-about 30 of us
We all camped in the no power section, we booked that whole part out and it was great!

Really big beautiful lake that u can swim and do some kayaking in! Spent most of my time there in the water lol

Dogs are welcome on leashes, there are places to charge your phone-super great bc u can leave them there on charge throughout the day and get it back when u need.
BBQs and kitchens/communal rooms are in each section.
Giant footy oval and playground and outdoor theatre there
There is also a great little tuck shop/cafe there, everything is super cheap especially the lollies!

Super cool peacocks everywhere that u can get a great pic with!

Super good for the price and I will definitely will be going again!!!

Great spot close to town, but still in lovely bushland. We took a caravan so can't comment on the cabin although they look good. Lovely people who run the park. Very popular place for a weekend or longer. Lots of Families stay at park. Oh lots of ducks, Lia birds walking round place.

"Can you please close the door?"
That's what the male staff member half-shouted at us twice within 10 seconds.
We had entered the office/cafe, which was relatively narrow, and we just wanted to ask other members of our group if they wanted anything from the cafe.
The male staff member, who was too busy talking with another guy and didn't even say a simple "hi" to us, just yelled it twice.
You didn't swear at us, but I would categorise your behaviour as rude — a totally different level of attitude compared to the lady who introduced the park to us the day before.
I don't know who you are, but you'd better not do the same to others.

Other than that, the place was nice.
Great spot right on the lake. The facilities including the showers were clean and excellent. It was a nice touch having a small cafe with ice creams and other essentials on site. Staff were lovely.
Love atmosphere and cabin is so clean and cozy
The manager and groundskeeper were great. We had a great private campsite with all sorts of birds around the tent. Nice walks and a fire pit hire

I arrived home just now from 5 days at Lake Jubilee Caravan park. I booked 3 cabins for my kids and a powered site for my wife and I. It is a marvellous park and we had a wonderful time. The staff are great, I had a drive through site for our 21f van. We met some wonderful people. Lots to do! Some lovely walks around the lakes, we went for a drive to Castlemaine and we went to Malden. There are markets, and the Daylesford shops are what we love. It's was a great getaway and we will be back!
Absolutely amazing and beautiful fresh air wildlife  disability friendly
Really nice, relaxed, natural campground, clean toilets/showers, enjoyed our brief stay -havecalready rebooked  - can't comment about accommodation as stayed in our own rooftop tent.
Lovely place to stay, great service

Beautiful area of the world.
Site was large.
Amenities fine.
Peacocks kept the snakes away.
Very good customer service, relax atmosphere and comfortable stay.
Lovely place to visit. Peacocks are demonstrating beautifully 😍 🤩 👌 ❣️ ❤️ I love to go back to this place.
Took our caravan for a few days, wonderful place, wildlife was amazing and walking around the lake was great.

What a magical and idyllic caravan park this is. Top spot and the people/family that run Jubilee Lake caravan park are amazing and can't do enough for you and it's beautifully kept. Well done. Highly recommend. 👍👌👏👏

Male staff member rude. Bathrooms were not being cleaned, soap was empty everyday, sanitary bins were so full people had started piling them on top of the bins. I called reception to let them know and the bare minimum was done. Peacocks were cute.

Ignore the prior review to mine. We were there today and Faran was an extremely belligerent individual with no concept of water safety. When asked to calm down, he became extremely aggressive toward Chris, who was just doing his job in a professional manner. If you can't listen to safety instructions, stay away from water!

This was our second time back, we brought the kids this time. It's a bloody great spot. We hired the paddle boats and the kids had an absolute ball. The price is fantastic too!

Just glad it's far enough away from Melbourne that there aren't too many rude, entitled people there. Happily going back!

Went to hire the boat with family.
The maintenance guy Chris was extremely rude to us and asked us to stay back in an extremely rude manner.
He refused us to have us on board.
The manager was also very unhelpful.
Won't recommend this to anyone
Great place

Need to sort out online system for tent bookings - had to call quite a few times to book only to get through to an annoyingly patronising guy on the phone. Other than that loved the lake and the peacocks. Amenities were average but clean.
Loved the peacocks! Cute little park and very reasonably priced.
Great place for a picnic. Beautiful friendly Peacocks. Amazing colours 🦚🦚💙💚
The Park was very quiet and relaxing.
Amazing place in daylesford
Super walk around the lake today. So unspoilt. Highly recommend. Beautiful spot.

Jubilee Lake is a great spot for families! Took the kids out, and they loved exploring the trails and playing by the water. Plenty of space, clean facilities, and a relaxing atmosphere. Perfect for a family day out!

Great caravan park with excellent amenities with great hot showers. Generous size sites with good shade cover. Ample things to do from walking to fishing to local shopping only a few minutes away. Bonus of Cafe on site with good range on offer. Will definitely be back again and will recommend to friends. Plus dog friendly
Peace an bird Noisy loved every minute
Over priced very simple cabins- incredibly clean, though st that price being expected to wash out own dishes was a stretch. We had fun around our own fire pit.

A lovely escape from the city. With a hot shower, toilet and camping kitchen facilities.

The abundance of peacocks is a delightful bonus.

Did a quick stop for refreshments before going back to Melbourne. Bathroom was clean and well maintained. There is a canoe ride service, but didn't have time to try that. However saw familes enjoying the lake, parks, playground.
Had a lovely stay once again,  the gentleman in the office was always cheery and happy to chat

It was a lovely place our site though was to close to main road which on Saturday and Sunday was crazy busy with tourists driving past, and having picnics and smelly smokers ( so not very isolated ) but overall a nice little caravan park and close to Daylesford township.
Love a walk around Jubilee Lake.  Highly recommended.

Lovely place.  Was disappointed at winter prices.  Bit expensive.  Heaters did not come on in shower blocks.  Try having a shower in a concrete block at 2 degrees however they Used too. And peacock poo everywhere.  Nothing worse than stepping that thru vans, units. Won’t be back after being here so many times I feel disappointed
Beautiful place with lots of autumn colours, playground, picnic area, clean bathroom, hot plates and many beautiful spots and walking track..

設備很齊全只是老舊一點
很不錯
Beautiful spot with awesome surrounds. Quiet and comfortable with clean amenities. Lovely big lake with some great walks around.

A beautiful place to relax and enjoy some spectacular scenery and the local wild life.
A brilliant kid and pet friendly campsite.
Such a beautiful relaxing place.

Booked a 4 night stay on a powered site with dogs coming along. Access to power and water was close, and toilets were a definite step up from some places we've been, just a little far, you know, if you needed to get there in a hurry. The walks were easy but not wheelchair friendly. Did see some people with motorised scooters navigating the loose gravel steep drives in the park, this park has a few sites with level ground, but it is an elevated site and almost no effort has been made to level off the sites that were around us. We were sold bail but not told there were no fish in the lake, google has all but ignored this place in terms of searching for takeaway food, you need to scroll down and use the term, daylesford takeaway, to get results. The fish and chips special: trout and chips for $11 was a winner, and the staff there at The Seafood Bar, were fantastic and friendly. Only found out where to fish on the last night so went home disapointed about that.
Always a great experience. Great staff and well maintained facilities.

Basic facilities are available, however quite limited amenities given size of park. Our campsite was nice, but some others looked a bit rough and dusty. Nice trees but no direct sunlight. Generators are not allowed in the park, so be prepared it may be difficult to keep batteries charged.

Cafe food and service were good and reasonably priced. No fish to be caught, and the ducks kept sobotaging our efforts. Will book again, Happy Campers.
Good place for children, water was clean, peacocks running around,
Great park. A must for family and friends
Fabulous location.  Quiet except for bird noises.  Good clean amenities.  I would definitely stay again if in this area
A beautiful park, great service and dog friendly.   It was the perfect weekend getaway.  Kim in the office was fantastic.  Nothing was too hard.  We will definitely be back.
Good to walk around lake and picnic spot

A few days ago, my friend and I came to this place, it was the first time I came to this place, and I saw the behavior of the employees there that I was really surprised and sad! They changed our place without a reasonable and convincing explanation!!! Our place was next to an Australian family and they said you should move out of here!! Their behavior was extremely racist and dictatorial!! They were really rude. I will never come to this place again!!
Always a beautiful location to just sit by the water. The peacocks are friendly and gorgeous walks
Well maintained park.
Loved the wild life

Good coffee great views
Lovely spacious dog friendly caravan and cabins.

Visited this lake on new years day. I observed many people with tent set ups and same caravans. Looks like a great place to spend camping. Lake has canoe and foot pedal boats. Some hiking trails. Decent playground and peacocks of all things roaming around. Kind of surreal. Cool place to visit for a couple if hours and have a picnic.
Very cold but a beutiful spot for a family picnic.
My childhood family gateway  destination and now my own family local lake entertainment park.

We went for a day trip with our family friends, who were camping on the facility. Great place to be with the nature, watching peacocks, ducks, hearing cocaburra laughs. Kids playground was blast for our kids as well as the walk on the trail. Definitely will go for camping next time.
Called in for coffee but have stayed here many times in the past nice quiet great scenery very peaceful.
Lovely location. Amazing peacocks
Beautiful quiet surrounds to escape the hustle and bustle of Daylesford.

I was at the Caravan powered site, for 4 days.
The location was amazing. Wild life and the lake were unbelievable.
The map of the place was hard to find and understand, the place maintenance are very poor, very muddy and unpleasant just with a little bit of maintenance the place could be much better.
Can’t get any service as the phone is constantly busy.
Please let us know if this place is going to get some maintenance work for the future camping.
beautiful lake and park. Love the nature and beautiful birds!
Здоровый туризм -это круто.
Nice Location close to town
Travelling in a caravan.  New management.  Great spot to base camp and explore
I've been here several times before, I love the birds.   This time I could not get Internet and heating was very poor. I left one day early
I'd go back there
You can see peacock

This place was incredible! We came last minute to see if we could get a cabin for two - amazing spot, great prices, amenities were perfect, very clean and there was beautiful wildlife such as peacocks and even a hidden waterfall around the lake! A ten minute drive to the hot springs and 5 min drive to the town centre. We will for sure be back to try out camping here. One of my favourite campsites in VIC! Thank you for letting us stay.
Worthwhile a stay at and service good and staff very helpful.
Its a beautiful place and has a kids play area. There is also a walking track along the creek which is wonderful. An open space fire place is also available.
